model_response:
The sentence can be simplified by stripping away the nested negations.

1. Let P = “The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.”

2. “It is NOT the case that P” = ¬P.

3. “It is NOT true that ¬P” = ¬¬P = P.

4. “It is NOT the case that P” (again) = ¬P.

5. “It is NOT correct that ¬P” = ¬¬P = P.

Hence the whole statement is equivalent to P.

Since P is false in reality (the Great Wall is not generally visible to the naked eye from space), the overall statement is false.

False
